active_alerts.description: Please see station personnel or use a station call box to request assistance.
Please use nearby Wonderland Elevators 701 or 702.

Skipped outbound stops:
Tremont St opp St Mary's High School
Market St @ Tremont St

This is to accommodate track improvement work and the replacement of 14,000 railroad ties and is scheduled to complete mid-May, 2026.

Single-track operations will be in effect throughout this period so boarding areas may change.

Temporarily Suspended Inbound Trains:
•	126 (8:49am from Newburyport)
•	1132 (10:53am from Beverly)
•	46 (1:47pm from Rockport)
•	1150 (3:23 from Beverly)

Temporarily Suspended Outbound Trains:
•	125 (8:35am to Newburyport)
•	1129 (9:35am to Beverly)
•	39 (12:05pm to Rockport)
•	11:47am (2:05pm to Beverly)
Please see station personnel or use a station call box to request assistance.

Affected routes:
426
429
435
441
442
455
To board or exit, use the accessible walkway that connects the station entrance with Prospect St.

There are accessible mini-high platforms at the Western end of both the Track 1 and Track 2 sides. However, the existing pedestrian path between the two sides is long and includes steep slopes, especially on the Crest Road bridge over the tracks. Designs are underway to provide for a fully compliant pathway.

To exit, exit the train at Harvard, then take an Ashmont or Braintree-bound train to Central and use Central Elevator 861. To board, cross Mass Ave to the Route 1 bus stop at Mass Ave and Prospect St, and take a Route 1 (Harvard) bus to Harvard to access the Red Line.
Scheduled to complete sometime in 2027, the Reconstruction of Foster Street includes widening and resurfacing with the addition of bicycle lanes.

Skipped outbound stops:
136 Georgetowne Pl

Skipped inbound stops:
136 Georgetowne Pl

To travel from the North Station platform to the Fitchburg platform, travel east on Maple St towards Main St. At Main St, turn left and continue to Railroad St. At Railroad St, turn left and continue to the station, where a ramp to the Fitchburg platform is located next the small parking lot. To travel from the Fitchburg platform to the North Station platform, travel to the east end of the platform and proceed down the ramp to the small parking lot. Continue east on Railroad St to Main St. At Main St, turn right and continue to Maple St. At Maple St, turn right and continue to the walkway that leads to the platform.

Affected lines:
Foxboro Event Service
Providence/Stoughton Line

Affected routes:
60
65

To exit, travel down the nearby ramp to the Orange Line Oak Grove platform, turn left, then use State Elevator 967 or 802 to access the Wonderland platform. Continue along the Wonderland platform to the 53 State St lobby and use State Elevator 975 to access the street. To board, cross State St, then proceed to the station entrance at 53 State St and use State Elevator 975 to access the Wonderland platform. At the Wonderland platform, turn left and proceed to the end of the platform (follow signs for Orange Line and Government Center/Bowdoin) and use State Elevator 967 or 802 to access the Orange Line Oak Grove platform. At the Oak Grove platform, turn right and travel up the nearby ramp to the Blue Line Bowdoin platform.
Please use nearby ramp to the platform.

Affected lines:
Fall River/New Bedford Line

Skipped outbound stops:
Oxford St @ Washington St
Oxford St @ Market St

Ashmont branch riders:
Regular service will operate between JFK/UMass and Ashmont, but riders must take a shuttle bus between Broadway and JFK/UMass.

Accessibility:
Free and accessible shuttle buses will operate between Broadway and Quincy Center. Accessible van service will be available between Broadway and Quincy Center on Monday, May 4. Riders should see station personnel for assistance.

Alternate service:
Riders are encouraged to consider Commuter Rail service between South Station and Braintree, which will be free during this service change. Regular fares should be purchased for travel beyond Braintree.

Travel times:
Riders using shuttles bus service should budget ample extra travel time. For example, a rider travelling to Park Street from Braintree should budget at least an additional 40 – 50 minutes of travel in additional to their regular commute.

Shuttle Locations:

Broadway:
W. Broadway @ Broadway Station

Andrew:
Northbound - Dorchester Ave @ Leeds St
Southbound - Dorchester Ave @ Dexter St

JFK/UMass:
Northbound - Inner Busway
Southbound - Outer Busway

North Quincy:
All shuttles - North Quincy Busway

Wollaston:
All shuttles - Newport Ave @ Brook St

Quincy Center:
Burgin Pkwy @ Quincy Ctr Station

The escalator will remain out of service for the foreseeable future. This decision was made after a thorough inspection determined that corrosion on the escalator’s steel truss was so severe that it could not be repaired further and that a complete replacement of the escalator would be required. Replacing the escalator at this location requires a more extended unit meeting modern escalator safety requirements. This longer unit will require significant alteration to the station entrance to meet code requirements. The MBTA has currently begun design work to understand what station upgrades will be needed.

Due to these complexities, we expect the escalator will remain out of service for a significant time. We recognize the challenges this poses to our riders, and please know we are working to identify a solution as quickly as possible. In the meantime, Government Center Station, which is fully accessible and serves the Blue Line, is under a quarter-mile from Bowdoin Station.
